label: Salesforce
description: Read and write objects in your Salesforce org — Leads, Accounts, Contacts, Opportunities, custom objects.

form.title: Connect Salesforce
form.subtitle: Authorize Anton to read and update your Salesforce org.

methods.label: Sign in with Salesforce
methods.description: OAuth 2.0 web-server flow with PKCE — a browser pops open for you to log into your Salesforce org and grant Anton scoped access. Currently requires you to register a Connected App in your org (steps below); we'll switch to a hosted client in a future release.
methods.how_to:
## Sign in with Salesforce (OAuth 2.0)

The modern, recommended path. Anton spawns a tiny local server, opens Salesforce's login + consent screen in your browser, and exchanges the resulting authorization code for a refresh token — never sent to Anton's servers.

### Pattern A — using Anton's hosted Connected App

When the connector spec ships with a `client_id`, you don't see any fields here. Click **Submit**, your browser opens to your Salesforce login (production or sandbox), you grant the requested scopes, and Anton stores a refresh token locally.

### Pattern B — bring your own Connected App

If this method shows two fields below (**Consumer key** and **Consumer secret**), Anton doesn't ship a hosted Connected App — register your own:

1. Sign in to Salesforce as an admin → **Setup** (gear icon, top right) → **App Manager**.
2. Click **New Connected App** at the top right.
3. Fill in **Connected App Name**, **API Name**, and **Contact Email**.
4. Tick **Enable OAuth Settings**, then:
   - **Callback URL**: `http://127.0.0.1:8000/callback` (Anton picks a free loopback port at runtime; Salesforce matches the host part — if your org rejects the wildcard port, set up a few common ones like 8000, 8001, 8765 separated by newlines).
   - **Selected OAuth Scopes**: add **Manage user data via APIs (`api`)**, **Perform requests at any time (`refresh_token`, `offline_access`)**.
   - Tick **Require Secret for Web Server Flow** and **Require Secret for Refresh Token Flow**.
   - Tick **Require Proof Key for Code Exchange (PKCE)**.
5. Save. Salesforce takes a few minutes to provision the app — wait for it.
6. Open the Connected App, click **Manage Consumer Details** (you may need to verify identity), and copy the **Consumer Key** and **Consumer Secret** into the fields below.
7. Click **Submit**. Anton runs the same browser flow with your Connected App.

### Sandbox vs production

The spec defaults to `login.salesforce.com` (production). For a sandbox, change `auth_url` and `token_url` to `https://test.salesforce.com/services/oauth2/...` in the connector JSON, or maintain a parallel `salesforce_sandbox` connector.

### Revoking later

Admins can revoke at **Setup → Connected Apps OAuth Usage**. Individual users can revoke their own grants at **Personal Information → Connections**. The next task that hits Salesforce will fail with an auth error and prompt you to re-authorize.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Consumer key (Client ID)
fields.description: From your Connected App → Manage Consumer Details.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: Consumer secret (Client secret)
fields.description: Paired with the Consumer key; same Manage Consumer Details panel.

methods.label: Username + password (legacy)
methods.description: Salesforce's username-password OAuth flow — paste your username, password, security token, and Connected App credentials. Salesforce is deprecating this for new orgs; prefer the browser flow above unless you're scripting against a legacy environment.
methods.how_to:
## Username-password flow (legacy)

Salesforce calls this the **OAuth 2.0 Username-Password flow**. It's being deprecated for new orgs in favor of the web-server flow above. Use it only when:

- Your org disables interactive logins (CI/CD scripts).
- You're hitting a legacy environment that doesn't support PKCE.

You STILL need a Connected App with API scopes enabled — the username-password just replaces the browser step.

### Steps

1. Set up a Connected App following steps 1-6 in the **Sign in with Salesforce** how-to above.
2. In the Connected App, also tick **Permitted Users → All users may self-authorize** (or **Admin approved users are pre-authorized** + assign your user) AND **Allow OAuth Username-Password Flows** under the **API (Enable OAuth Settings)** section.
3. Get a **Security token** — Salesforce → your profile menu (top right) → **Settings → My Personal Information → Reset My Security Token**. Salesforce emails the new token to your account email.
4. Paste **Username**, **Password**, **Security token**, **Consumer key**, and **Consumer secret** below.

### Notes

- The password Anton stores is your raw Salesforce password concatenated with the security token — that's how Salesforce's username-password flow expects it.
- If your org enforces IP restrictions, you may not need the security token; for safety, fill it anyway.
- Read [Salesforce's username-password flow guide](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.remoteaccess_oauth_username_password_flow.htm) — note the deprecation banners.

fields.name: username
fields.label: Salesforce username
fields.description: Your Salesforce login (the email-shaped one, including any sandbox suffix like .sandbox).

fields.name: security_token
fields.label: Security token
fields.description: From Salesforce → My Personal Information → Reset My Security Token. Salesforce emails it to you.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: Consumer key (Client ID)
fields.description: From your Connected App → Manage Consumer Details.
